national_expectations.summary: After reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time in their history, the bar has shifted from simply surviving the group to genuinely going further; Ivorian and pan-African press now frame the Elephants as a credible dark horse and openly talk of pushing the adventure as far as possible rather than treating the round of thirty-two as the ceiling.

national_mood.summary: The mood is celebratory and proud, tipping into real belief: Ivorian outlets describe enthusiastic media and a smiling, joyful squad after the historic Curacao win, with players sharing their delight, while the coach pairs respect for opponents with openly stated ambition. There is also a sober undercurrent because the next opponent carries one of the tournament's most feared attacks, so optimism is paired with respect rather than complacency.

manager_situation.summary: Emerse Fae's standing is very high. The continental-title winner who took over mid-tournament in twenty twenty-four has now become the first coach to carry Ivory Coast beyond a World Cup group stage, and press credit his tactical flexibility and rotation across a notably young squad. He pairs evident pride in the achievement with respect for opponents and openly stated ambition for the next round.

historical_context.summary: Ivory Coast had reached the World Cup three times before and been eliminated in the group stage on each occasion, so this run is their first ever appearance in the knockout rounds. Their broader pedigree rests on continental success, including the African title won after Emerse Fae stepped up from assistant mid-tournament to become the first manager to win the continental crown after taking charge during the competition.

dominant_storylines.storyline: Nicolas Pepe's redemption: the previously much-maligned former Arsenal winger, recalled to the starting line-up after being benched against Germany, scored both goals in the Curacao win that sealed the historic qualification, and is now cast as a decisive, in-form figure for the knockout tie.

A history-making coach and a young squad: Emerse Fae, the continental-title winner, has become the first to take Ivory Coast past the group stage, with press praising his rotation and tactical flexibility (starting many different players and switching shape mid-match) across a notably youthful group, with veteran captain Franck Kessie anchoring midfield.

Dark-horse counter-attacking identity: previews build the tie around Ivory Coast's pace and directness through young winger Yan Diomande and Amad Diallo against Norway's feared attack, framing the flank battle involving Diomande and his RB Leipzig connection as potentially decisive.

Scoring-first consistency milestone: Ivory Coast opened the scoring in all three group games, described in previews as only the second African nation to do so at a World Cup, reinforcing the narrative of a side that imposes itself early.

Ivorian press frames Norway as the more winnable half of the draw: outlets stress that France, group winners, avoided a rematch with the Elephants (whom they lost to 2-1 in a pre-tournament friendly), leaving the Norwegians, described as comparatively unaccustomed to major international competitions, as the kinder round-of-32 opponent, though pieces add the caveat that no opponent should be underestimated at this stage.

The tie is framed by Ivorian outlets as a Haaland problem: KOACI casts the round-of-32 as the Norwegian challenge of Erling Haaland and names veteran captain Franck Kessie as the man who must front up to Haaland's side, presenting the Norwegian striker as the central test for an Elephants team that is celebrating but wary.

A confident not-afraid column from Abidjan: Afrikipresse argues the Elephants need not fear Norway, pointing to the side's collective strength, solidarity, tactical discipline and capacity for resilience as the basis for getting past a Norway team built around Haaland's attacking threat. (Page returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; framing captured only via search snippets, so treat as lower-confidence sourcing; the column also carries a prediction/scoreline that was excluded.)

Ivorian outlet relays Norwegian press respect for the Elephants: Zappingmedias reports Norwegian pundits view Ivory Coast as a clearly tougher proposition than Senegal, citing the Elephants leading Germany and holding on for a long time, while Norwegian media flag RB Leipzig winger Yan Diomande and his one-on-one ability down the left as the chief threat to Norway's defence, a cross-press angle underlining how seriously the opponent takes the tie.

attributed_quotes.quote: We respect all teams, but we have our ambitions.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: Nous respectons toutes les équipes, mais nous avons nos ambitions.
attributed_quotes.context: Looking ahead to the round-of-32 opponent (France or Norway, then unconfirmed) after the win over Curacao that sealed Ivory Coast's first World Cup knockout-stage qualification.

attributed_quotes.quote: France or Norway, we are preparing to win.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: La France ou la Norvège, on se prépare pour gagner !
attributed_quotes.context: Speaking after qualification, before the round-of-32 opponent was confirmed, expressing the squad's ambition for the knockout tie.

attributed_quotes.quote: We have no preference between Norway and France. We'll take whoever shows up and anyway, if we want to go far, we must beat them all.
attributed_quotes.quote_original: On n'a pas de préférence entre la Norvège et la France. On prendra l'équipe qui se présentera et de toute façon, si on veut aller loin, il faut toutes les battre.
attributed_quotes.context: Speaking after the Curacao win, before the round-of-32 opponent was confirmed as Norway, on his side's mindset for the knockouts.

local_availability_reporting.status_reported: Reported sidelined with a hamstring issue going into the knockout round; his readiness for the Norway tie is not confirmed.

Reported to have missed the entire group stage through injury but to have returned to training in recent days; his availability for the knockouts is described as an open question rather than confirmed.
Reported as a doubt after being substituted during the Curacao group game; his fitness for the round-of-32 match is described as uncertain in previews.
Reported as expected to return to the back four after missing the Curacao group match, though not officially confirmed in the starting line-up.

gaps: Could not source a verbatim quote from Emerse Fae specifically about the Norway tie or about facing Erling Haaland; the verified Fae quote is about respecting opponents and Ivory Coast's ambitions, given before the opponent was confirmed.

The draft's earlier Fae 'It's pride, it's joy' quote could not be confirmed at its cited URL and was dropped; replaced with the verified 'respect all teams... ambitions' quote from the same info.fr article.

No official Ivorian Football Federation (FIF) statement found in directly fetchable form; the Singo medical/recovery update relayed via Foot Africa returned HTTP 403 and could not be re-read, so the Singo item rests on the Sports Mole hamstring report.
Could not confirm the definitive starting XI or whether Amad Diallo, Singo, Ndicka or Agbadou will actually feature against Norway; only reported/uncertain status available.
Amad Diallo and Agbadou team-news lines come from Racing Post, a betting/tips outlet; retained as reported team news only (no odds/predictions used), but treat as lower-confidence sourcing.
No directly attributed quote from a named Ivorian journalist; the dark-horse and Pepe-redemption framings come from preview summaries rather than a signed columnist quote.

source_data_snapshot.used_for: Re-verified verbatim Emerse Fae 'no preference between Norway and France' quote (Ivorian national outlet)
Haaland-challenge framing (headline 'le defi norvegien d'Erling Haaland'), Kessie-vs-Haaland line, and the favourable-draw / Norway-fielded-B-team framing
France-avoided-the-Elephants / Norway-unaccustomed-to-major-competitions framing, 2-1 friendly win over France, and the no-underestimation caveat
'Meme pas peur' confidence/resilience/solidarity framing (page HTTP 403 on direct fetch; content via search snippets; prediction/scoreline excluded)
Ivorian relay of Norwegian press respect (CIV tougher than Senegal, led Germany); Diomande flagged as main threat
Cross-check of the Curacao 2-0 qualification and Pepe-as-architect framing (no new verbatim quote found)
